content:
First note that 

\[
S \;=\;9+9^2+\cdots+9^{2010}
\;=\;\frac{9\bigl(9^{2010}-1\bigr)}{9-1}
\;=\;\frac{9\bigl(9^{2010}-1\bigr)}{8}.
\]

A prime \(p\neq2\) divides \(S\) if and only if 
\[
9\,(9^{2010}-1)\equiv0\pmod p
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
9^{2010}\equiv1\pmod p
\quad(\text{unless }p=3\text{ which divides the 9 explicitly}). 
\]

We check the small primes in order:

2: one checks \(S\) is even, so \(2\mid S\).  
3: obviously \(3\mid9\mid S\).  
5: \(9\equiv4\pmod5\), and \(4^2\equiv1\pmod5\), so \(4^{2010}=(4^2)^{1005}\equiv1\), hence \(5\mid S\).  
7: \(9\equiv2\pmod7\), and \(2^3\equiv1\pmod7\) with \(3\mid2010\), so \(7\mid S\).  
11: \(\operatorname{ord}_{11}(9)=5\mid2010\), so \(11\mid S\).  
13: \(\operatorname{ord}_{13}(9)=3\mid2010\), so \(13\mid S\).  

Next is \(p=17\).  One computes \(\operatorname{ord}_{17}(9)=8\), and \(8\nmid2010\), so
\[
9^{2010}\not\equiv1\pmod{17},
\]
hence \(17\nmid S\).  

Therefore the smallest prime not dividing \(S\) is 

\[
\boxed{17}.
\]
